title: oxalis glabra
intro: Oxalis glabra, also known as the smooth woodsorrel, is a perennial plant belonging to the Oxalis genus. It is primarily found in the mountainous regions of Asia, where it thrives in damp, shaded areas. The leaves and stems of Oxalis glabra contain oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including treating rheumatism, gout, and digestive issues. The herbal preparation of Oxalis glabra is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting antioxidant activity.
